= Ruy Lopez: Four Knights Game = {{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Spanish Four Knights Game| |rd| |bd|qd|kd| | |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | |nd| | |nd| | |= | |bl|bd| |pd| | | |= | | | | |pl| | | |= | | |nl| | |nl| | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl| ||rl|= || }} If Bxc6 dxc6 Nxe5? is a mistake after Bxf2 targeting the weak f2 square by sacrificing the bishop. After Kxf2, Qd4+ forks the King and the Knight. After Ke2 Qxe5, White has a king which cannot castle in a relatively open position. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}} '''1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 Nc6 3. Nc3 Nf6 4. Bb4 Bc5'''
